item.title: Show HN: OpenAI-Compatible Cloudflare AI Endpoint Generator
item.description:
An OpenAI-compatible API generator backed by Cloudflare Workers AI — chat, speech, vision, embeddings — deployed to your account in one click and with a testing dashboard. No login, just need a Cloudflare account and a single-use CF token to deploy.

I'm a big fan of testing the latest models deployed on Cloudflare, and the 10,000 "neuron" allotment per day is pretty generous.

I built this to supplement a side project I've been working on for the last six months. https://notebooker.ai, which has a no-login demo using the models on Cloudflare. It's a hacked-on open-notebook in the cloud with apps to quickly save bookmarks and generate podcast RSS feeds. Features, like a "creator" plugin sdk, are available for use in https://github.com/Notebooker-ai. Not officially released yet but happy for any feedback.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48993220

Points: 1

# Comments: 0

item.title: Who am AI – interrogate an AI playing a secret character, 10 questions
Antares: Open Weight AI Models for Vulnerability Localization
The OWASP Agentic AI Top: What Builders on AWS Need to Know
Control-Theory Taxonomy of Physical AI
Where Your AI Lives Matters More Than How Smart It Is – Especially in the UAE
AI Is ThoughtWare, Not Software: The End of Thinking as We Know It
Show HN: Rowset – An open-source back end for AI agents
Study Claiming AI Helps Students Learn Retracted
AI Agents Should Propose Infrastructure, Not Mutate It
AI Companies Are Buying Tons of Old Books Because They're Free of AI Slop
Gumroad spent equal amounts on AI tokens and human payroll
Show HN: Maith – open math research with AI
item.description:
If AI got lucky or not, AI is surely going to assist in solving serious math problems. If you want to spend your resources/weekends contributing, this repository is full of math problems where you can add your thoughts (or proofs - for you Good Will Hunting type people) for others to build on it and we all together can try to change the world of math.

I have already extended the research with manual proof of Jacobian Conjecture that was posted on Twitter (with attribution).

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48992161

Points: 5

# Comments: 0

item.title: Show HN: TZRO – A Free Local AI Task Offloader
AI physics on vacation turned into real research in quantum mechanics
Contra George Hotz on "AI 2040 and the Cult of Intelligence"
Ask HN: To lead with, or bury, AI in product marketing
item.description:
Howdy dear folks.

Here on hackernews there seems to be very little other than AI related stories. Maybe not all too strange given the crowd who frequents this site. From what I gather the mostly positive sentiment isn't as widely shared among the general population (0: 50% of americans are more concerned than excited about the increase use of AI).

How do you think about whether or not to mention, or advertise, the AI capabilities of your own products when marketing to the general population? Do you advertise them heavily, sell them as a feature, or just let them add value and fade into the background as any other implementation detail?

I am very torn on this myself. We have recently added quite some AI features to our search products [1], namely ai generated answers, and using ai vision to classify images and the likes, and while these are features I am very proud of, I am rather hesitant to put them front and center in marketing, lest I alienate the more non-tech crowd that really is our core customer base.

Again: I'd love to hear how you think about, and approach, this in your own product marketing!

Lots of love from Berlin

0. https://www.pewresearch.org/science/2025/09/17/how-americans-view-ai-and-its-impact-on-people-and-society/ 1: https://monocle-search.com and https://searchcue.com

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48992019

Points: 2

# Comments: 0

item.title: Formal verification might solve AI's review bottleneck
Show HN: Neverbell, an AI agent that analyzes markets and executes trades
item.description:
Hi HN, we're developing Neverbell, an AI agent that combines market analysis with trade execution.

One of our beta testers said to Neverbell: “Protect my downside, but don’t be too conservative.”

Volatility increased a few days later, and the agent decreased their exposure.

Our tester wasn’t happy.

The interesting part for us was that both interpretations were reasonable.

The agent gave more weight to “protect my downside.”

The user thought that “don't be too conservative” was more important.

It changed the way we built Neverbell.

Now, if the agent sees any instructions that conflict, it will ask rather than guess what the user was trying to say.

We've seen this several times before; so we made it part of the product.

Interested in what others are doing.

Once an agent starts taking actions instead of just answering questions, where do you draw the line?

Will also answer questions about Neverbell, permissions, or anything else we've learned during the beta period.

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48991968

Points: 4

# Comments: 2

item.title: Show HN: Pinpoint – Visual feedback for AI coding agents
item.description:
I built Pinpoint because I was tired of typing out feedback on the visual work my AI agent did. I want my agent to be autonomous, so having it ask for feedback through a UI felt genuinely better. Dropping a contextual comment on each screenshot is much closer to how you'd give feedback on a design in Figma. It also does side-by-side comparison, which is really helpful, having the before/after screens lets the human in the loop (us!) give better feedback.

It's just screenshots and comments, the same way Plannotator is just a plan and comments, and that's still a successful product. I think there's real value in building a decent UX for this. Chat is the current paradigm for AI agents, but it doesn't have to be, and this is my attempt at changing that.

There's nothing wildly clever going on here. It just works really nicely for me, the same way Plannotator does. Would love your feedback.

No install demo here: https://pinpoint.maferland.com/review/try-demo Repo: https://github.com/maferland/pinpoint

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48991866

Points: 2

# Comments: 1

item.title: The AI Slot Machine Effect: Why Generative Feeds Disrupt Deep Work And How to Reclaim Focus
item.description:
You open a generative AI tool expecting a quick boost. Ten minutes later, you’re still there, refining a prompt for the fourth time. The task you started with has drifted off to the side somewhere. Sound familiar? Knowledge workers in 2026 are running into this more and more. It makes sense once you look at […]

The post The AI Slot Machine Effect: Why Generative Feeds Disrupt Deep Work And How to Reclaim Focus appeared first on AI News.
